name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description:
Project Layout is a Java utility library for detecting, describing, and working with conventional on-disk project directory layouts (sources, resources, tests, docs, and modules) in a consistent way. It targets build tooling and plugins that need reliable discovery of well-known folders across heterogeneous repositories.

Core API: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout models a project rooted at a filesystem directory and exposes root-relative paths for production sources, tests, and documentation, and optionally nested modules. The library includes utilities to compute stable root-relative paths and to traverse files/directories while excluding common build/tooling folders (e.g., node_modules, .git, target, build).

Layout detection/orchestration: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager detects an appropriate layout implementation for a given project directory by inspecting build metadata files and indicators.

Provided layout implementations:
- MavenProjectLayout: reads pom.xml (via PomReader) to determine modules for pom-packaging aggregators and to infer/compute source and test roots using Maven Build defaults and configuration.
- GragleProjectLayout: uses the Gradle Tooling API to load the GradleProject model, exposing child module names for multi-project builds and conventional directories (src/main, src/test, src/site).
- JScriptProjectLayout: parses package.json workspaces and resolves module directories by matching workspace glob patterns against folders containing package.json.
- PythonProjectLayout: detects Python projects via pyproject.toml metadata (and related heuristics) and currently returns safe empty defaults for layout paths.
- DefaultProjectLayout: filesystem-based fallback that treats immediate subdirectories as potential modules while excluding known tooling/build directories.

Key capabilities include project type detection (Maven/Gradle/Node.js/Python), module enumeration where supported, and consistent handling of root-relative path computation to support downstream scanners and processors.

Notable runtime dependencies include Maven Model APIs for pom parsing, Gradle Tooling API for Gradle model access, Jackson for JSON parsing, and tomlj for TOML parsing, plus commons-lang3/commons-io and slf4j for utilities/logging.

usageContext: Tooling that needs to locate sources/tests/docs across different project types

constructors.description: Creates a Maven-aware layout. Configure it with projectDir(java.io.File) before calling accessors.

examples.description: Create a MavenProjectLayout for a repository root and read conventional source roots.

constructors.description: Detects and instantiates the appropriate ProjectLayout (Maven/Gradle/JS/Python/Default) based on files present in the given directory, and sets the projectDir on the returned layout.

examples.description: Detect the layout type from a project directory and enumerate modules if supported.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects a project layout by inspecting build metadata files (pom.xml, build.gradle, package.json, pyproject.toml) and returns a configured ProjectLayout implementation (including a filesystem-based default).

examples.description: Detect a layout and obtain a short type name derived from the implementing class.

features.name: getRelativePath
features.signature: java.lang.String org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout.getRelativePath(java.io.File dir, java.io.File file, boolean addSingleDot)
features.description: Computes a normalized root-relative path from a project directory to a target file, with an option to prefix './'. Returns '.' when the target is the base directory; returns null when the computed result indicates the target is not within the project directory (as per current implementation behavior).

examples.description: Compute a root-relative path for a file under the project root.

features.name: findDirectories
features.description: Recursively enumerates directories under a project root while excluding common build/tooling directories (e.g., node_modules, .git, target, build). Useful for lightweight repository scanning and workspace module resolution.

examples.snippet:
java.io.File root = new java.io.File("/repo");
java.util.List<java.io.File> dirs = org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout.findDirectories(root);
examples.description: Traverse project directories while skipping typical noisy folders.

features.name: getModules (Maven)
features.description: For Maven projects with packaging 'pom', returns modules declared in pom.xml; otherwise returns null (indicating no module enumeration for non-aggregator projects).

examples.description: Read declared Maven modules from an aggregator pom.xml.

features.name: getModules (Gradle)
features.description: Uses the Gradle Tooling API GradleProject model to enumerate child projects for multi-project builds; returns empty list if the model cannot be loaded or has no children.

examples.description: List Gradle child modules using the tooling model.

features.name: getModules (JavaScript workspaces)
features.description: Parses package.json workspaces and resolves module directories by matching workspace glob patterns against directories containing package.json, returning root-relative module paths.

examples.description: Resolve Yarn/npm/pnpm workspace modules from package.json.

examples.title: Scan a repository and process modules recursively
examples.description: Use ProjectProcessor to detect the layout, recurse into modules when reported, and handle each project folder via processFolder(ProjectLayout).
